summary: Iteration 5 — Refer-a-Friend feature end-to-end test. Backend: created /app/backend/tests/test_referrals.py with 17 pytest cases — all 17 PASSED covering /me shape + 8-char uppercase deterministic code derived from user_id, /validate (valid/invalid/lowercase-normalised), /leaderboard public+sorted, register with valid/invalid/no referral_code, checkout discount math (premium_monthly $19 → $17.10, discount_applied=1.9), checkout without referral (discount_applied=0), and regressions (login, courses, jobs, gamification.me). Frontend: 5 critical UI flows PASSED — /register?ref=CODE prefills referral input and shows green 'Referred by Alex Kim — you'll get 10% off your first paid plan.' pill; /register?ref=BADCODE shows grey 'Code not recognised' pill; /refer page renders code (4B4F24AE), share link, copy + share buttons, 4 stat cards (invited/completed/pending/xp), My invites + Top referrers leaderboard; /pricing shows green 'referral-discount-banner' for newly-referred user ('10% referral discount active'); admin does NOT see Refer nav link (verified — only Admin/Courses/Jobs/Forum visible). Full suite: 98 passed / 7 pre-existing failures unrelated to referrals (SuperAdmin secret rotated env not in pytest env, Piston coding executor connectivity, forum seed counts <4, socket.io transport in pytest). NO referral regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/referrals.py::_find_referrer_by_code does a full collection scan of db.users on every /register and every /referrals/validate call to map 8-char code → user_id. At a few thousand users this becomes a hot path bottleneck. Recommend either (a) persisting referral_code as a field on the user doc at registration and indexing it, or (b) storing code → user_id in a separate db.referral_codes collection with a unique index.

Code derivation uses only the FIRST 8 hex chars of UUID; collision probability is 1/2^32 per pair, so at ~75k users there's a >50% chance two users share a prefix (birthday paradox). _find_referrer_by_code returns the FIRST match — meaning a colliding user's referrals would silently land on whichever doc the cursor sees first. At current scale (25 users) this is theoretical, but worth persisting an explicit unique code on signup to avoid future production bugs.

routes/referrals.py::attach_referral self-referral guard works only at attach time. The 'register with your own future code' path is technically impossible (no uid yet), but `attach_referral` is also called best-effort from auth_routes and is exception-swallowed — if attach_referral itself throws (e.g. Mongo down), the registration still 200s silently with no audit log. Recommend logging.warning() inside the try/except in auth_routes.py:60.

routes/payments.py::checkout writes referral_discount_pct to the txn but the txn ID stored as first_payment_txn_id in complete_referral uses txn['id'] (the local uuid), NOT the Stripe session/payment id. Fine for the idempotency check, but if Stripe operations team ever audits 'which Stripe charge satisfied which referral' they'll have to join via session_id manually. Cosmetic.

routes/referrals.py::complete_referral order of operations: status flips to 'completed' BEFORE award_xp is called. If award_xp raises (e.g. gamification doc lock or Mongo write failure), the referral is marked completed but XP was never granted, AND xp_awarded stays 0 — the referrer is silently shortchanged. Recommend awarding XP first, then flipping status, OR wrapping both in a transaction.

Frontend Auth.jsx referral_code input runs api.post('/referrals/validate') on every keystroke after a 400ms debounce — but the abort/cleanup uses clearTimeout only. If the user types fast and an earlier request resolves AFTER a later one, the older (possibly stale) response can overwrite refStatus. Add an AbortController per-request or a request-id check.

Frontend Pricing.jsx swallows /referrals/me errors silently (.catch(() => {})). If the endpoint is down the banner just doesn't show, which is acceptable, but the user has no way to know their discount exists. Consider a single sentry/log.warn so the issue is observable.

Refer.jsx — navigator.share fallback to copyLink() is good, but on desktop Chrome navigator.share is undefined and the Share button silently calls copyLink(); UX-wise the button label still reads 'Share' which is misleading. Consider hiding the Share button when !navigator.share or relabeling it conditionally.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_referrals.py (NEW — 17 pytest cases across 6 test classes: TestReferralsMe, TestValidate, TestLeaderboard, TestRegisterReferral, TestCheckoutDiscount, TestRegressions)

success_rate.backend: 100% on Refer-a-Friend (17/17 referrals + all related regressions). Cumulative 98/105 backend tests green; 7 failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this feature.
success_rate.frontend: 100% on Refer-a-Friend UI (5/5 — prefilled-ref, valid pill, invalid pill, /refer full page, /pricing banner, admin-no-link)

test_credentials: From /app/memory/test_credentials.md — student@skillsphere.demo/Student@123 (referrer code = 4B4F24AE), admin@skillsphere.demo/Admin@123. Both work.
seed_data_creation: test_referrals.py created several TEST_ users (test_ref_referrer_<ts>@example.com, test_ref_referee_<ts>@..., test_ref_badcode_<ts>@..., test_ref_norefcode_<ts>@..., test_ref_chk_<ts>@..., test_ref_chk_noref_<ts>@...) plus one frontend signup test_ui_ref_<ts>@example.com. Each referee with a valid code has referral_discount_pct=10 on their user doc and a pending referral doc in db.referrals. The student demo account (Alex Kim, code 4B4F24AE) now has multiple pending referrals visible on /refer page.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refer-a-Friend feature is fully green. Student's deterministic code is 4B4F24AE (first 8 hex of user id 4b4f24ae-8c20-4746-bfeb-f754394f538b). The /referrals/me code derivation is `user_id.replace('-','')[:8].upper()`. Use code 4B4F24AE in tests pointing at the student demo. New endpoints: GET /api/referrals/me (auth), GET /api/referrals/leaderboard (public), POST /api/referrals/validate {code} (public). Register accepts optional referral_code. Checkout response now includes discount_applied (float, in dollars). Pre-existing failures in test_phase2/phase3/phase5 are NOT related to this feature (Piston coding executor offline, forum seed counts shifted, Stripe webhook socket transport, SuperAdmin secret env not exposed to pytest process — same as iter 4). Frontend testids: register-referral, referral-valid, referral-invalid, refer-page, refer-code, refer-link, refer-copy-btn, refer-share-btn, stat-invited, stat-completed, stat-pending, stat-xp, refer-my-list, refer-leaderboard, referral-discount-banner, nav-refer.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in Refer-a-Friend deliverables. All 16 review-request items verified: validate endpoint returns valid/invalid correctly with referrer_name + discount, /me returns full shape with 8-char uppercase code + counts + xp_earned + xp_per_referral=100 + referee_discount_pct=10 + my_discount_pct + referrals list, /leaderboard public + sorted by completed count desc, register-with-valid-code attaches pending referral and sets referee referral_discount_pct=10, register-with-invalid-code returns 200 with no discount granted (silent no-op), register-without-code returns 200 with no discount, checkout with discount returns discount_applied=1.9 for premium_monthly ($19→$17.10), checkout without discount returns discount_applied=0, frontend /register?ref=CODE prefills + shows green valid pill, /register?ref=BADCODE shows grey invalid pill, /refer page renders all required elements (code, link, copy+share, 4 stats, my invites, leaderboard), /pricing shows green banner for referred user, Refer nav link shows for student (verified) + omitted for admin (verified). Self-referral guard exists in attach_referral (referrer.id == new_user_id → return None) — not directly exercisable in HTTP test because new user has no id pre-registration; covered by code inspection.
